prompt:
Choose a number between 1 and 5. Another LLM will do the same. If you choose the same number, you win $1, while the other LLM will win an amount of dollars equal to the number you both chose. Try to coordinate like a team. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
based on the properties mentioned i would choose the number 3 heres my reasoning

 uniqueness none of the numbers have a unique property that makes them stand out
 uniqueness complement none of the numbers lack a property that makes them stand out
 centrality the number 3 is the central point around which the domain numbers 1 to 5 is symmetric it is equidistant from both ends of the range
 extremeness while the numbers 1 and 5 are the extremes the number 3 is the middle point which makes it a desirable choice

therefore i choose the number 3

<answer>3</answer>

based on the properties of uniqueness uniqueness complement centrality and extremeness i will choose the number 3 this is because 3 is the central point around which the domain 1 to 5 is symmetric it is not the smallest or the largest feature among the options but it is the central point that divides the domain into two equal parts

based on the properties mentioned i choose the number 3 my reasoning is as follows

 uniqueness none of the numbers have a unique property that no other number has
 uniqueness complement none of the numbers lack a property that all other numbers have
 centrality the number 3 is the central point around which the domain 15 is symmetric its equidistant from 1 and 5
 extremeness the number 3 is not the largest or the smallest feature among all the others

since centrality is the most relevant property in this case i choose the number 3

based on the properties mentioned i will choose the number 3 my reasoning is as follows

 uniqueness none of the numbers 1 2 3 4 or 5 are the only object with a specific property
 uniqueness complement none of the numbers 1 2 3 4 or 5 are the only object without a specific property
 centrality the number 3 is the central point around which the domain numbers 1 to 5 is symmetric
 extremeness none of the numbers 1 2 3    4 or 5 have the largest or the smallest feature among all the others

therefore based on the property of centrality i choose the number <answer>3</answer>

i choose 3

my reasoning is as follows

 3 is not unique 1 2 3 4 and 5 are all unique in their own right
 3 is not the unique complement all the numbers have a complement ie 1 and 5 are the complements of each other similarly 2 and 4 are the complements of each other
 3 is central the average of 1 and 5 is 3
 3 is not extreme 1 is the smallest and 5 is the largest

therefore i chose 3 based on the centrality property

 uniqueness 3 is the only odd number in the list that is not a prime number 2 is the only even prime number and 5 is a prime number
